note: both options are close, debuffs are normally weak but gust only does less than 10% so the chip is almost worthless. however multiple sand attacks would be worse, as geodude can switch out to remove all debuffs, so like 3 sand attacks cost 3 turns and geodude switches on turn to remove all debuffs. so sand attack then gust. switching to noctowl is interesting because noctowl can use special attacks to do more damage, but its not worth taking so much damage from geodude. better to sadn attack to weaken geodude then spam gust until you die for safe switch

if quilava is faster then wing attack is better, because otherwise it just goes ember > swords dance > ember and we die

but if scyther is faster, then it goes swords dance > ember > wing attack KOs. 

since wing attack without a boost doesnt Ko quilava, we have to take an ember either way. swords dancing first still kills quilava in 2 turns, taking only one ember, and leaves us with +2

both moves are good, and roll out is iconic. but since geodude has high defense and resists roll out, its best to body slam for extra damage and paralysis chance. once geodude is paralyzed, then switching to roll out is fine

both moves are good. clefairy takes 33% max so double team is a good set up once, but if it used reflect last turn and clefairy is faster then encore is a must click because it locks it into reflect which fails if its active already. only risk is them switching then encore does nothing. so if faster encore, if slower then double team once

destiny bond is a bad play here because you have to go first and takes down both (and relies on gengar dying, it could set up on you or switch or not do enough damage) when you have a move that does 300% damage! just take the kill, simple
never worry about being cheap, its a legal play. sleep is broken, do it.

focus punch is a straight up terrible move. the player is highly likely to attack here. ice punch is a really good move here, high damage and chance to kill. however an even better play is saving your HP when you have a rock type in the back to wall a flying normal type with huge defense
not much to say, higher expected damage and chance to KO
both are good. focus energy isnt a great set up move, if it had boosted its attack or special attack then maybe prioritize phazing with whirlwind
Slowking resists fire, so can probably switch in safely. cloyster is outsped by quilava and will die, otherwise exploding would be good. probably try saving cloyster to blow up later

piloswine cant be immune to ampharos due to dragon's majesty, but resists it then threatens ampharos with ground coverage. save slowking for better matchup. thunder wave is MAYBE ok if slowking would live a hit to neuter a giant threat in a dragon
think about it like this: without ice coverage, the player will switch out suicune because it cannot touch dragonair at all. so if they stay in its solely to go for ice and threaten dragonair
kingdra is fast but not the fastest pokemon. it needs one dragon dance and its faster than anything in the game. lapras cant threaten at all so good set up opportunity.

This one is just non-sensical. The point of Toxic is to help chip a pokemon slowly. Crobat just straight up kills Alakazam with Wing Attack. It's like choosing between earning $10 a day or getting a million dollars right now. You don't need the incremental damage of Toxic when you can bring it all the way to 0 immediately.

Genuinely ambiguous because hidden Ice coverage flips every move's value here. Dragon Dance is amazing if Suicune doesn't have Ice (with DD, Outrage's 3HKO becomes a 2HKO so no turn really lost setting up), but +2 Dragonite walks into a 4x Ice OHKO if it does. Outrage chips ~30% AND probes for Ice - if Suicune has it, the next turn reveals it; if not, that's safe chip to weaken Suicune for the rest of Lance's team. The current roster changes the upstream answer: instead of old Aerodactyl/Starmie pivots, Lance can scout or preserve through Kingdra, Gyarados, Steelix, Ampharos, or Yanma depending on public coverage risk. If forced at this moment, Outrage still tilts ahead for chip + information value, but the choice stays needs_context because the deeper lesson is ace timing and pivot selection before Dragonite is exposed.

Earthquake is definitely bad unless you predict a switch because Vaporeon will OHKO with any water move so may as well explode to trade. However staying in at all is a bad play because Vaporeon outspeeds and kills every time with water. Switching to Omastar is the best play, but I didn't suggest that because a below 50% HP slow pokemon like golem may sturggle to get value later as it will often always be outsped and so it's better to sacrifice it to get a clean switch into a full health Omastar rather than Omastar take chip damage switching in. And if the player overpredicts and doesn't use water move, Explosion will nail something. But Explosion and switch out are both very close in scoring and you should switch it up to avoid being too predictable.
Recover is a no go here, you will be outdamaged by super effective moves. Go ahead and get chip on it so Lapras can come in and kill with an ice move after a free switch in from Starmie fainting.

Same pattern as Erika: Sleep Powder first, then use the sleep turn to Quiver Dance, then attack with Giga Drain. If Lapras wakes up and Sleep Clause is free, Sleep Powder can be reapplied before continuing pressure.
